metadata.title:
CANNON WILL SUBMIT


mo JOKER" ORDINANCE

pages.raw_text:
The council will hear some more
about Northern Pacific grade separ-
alton this evening when the ordi-
nance asked by the railroad will
be taken up section by section.


Judge Turner will be on hand
again and this time will have more
individual support behind him than
on the last occasion. He will be
double-shotted for the fight against
the company's plan.


f. j. Cannon, n. p. cousel, will
also be there ana Cannon has a
proposition it will be hard to gel
around. In about II places in the
ordinance relating to the grade he
is inserting special provision that
all the tracks are to be elevated
and every paragraph so altered VIII
also contain the provision there
shall be no joker


This is to get around final objec-
ton entered that the company pro-
posed to elevate part of its tracks
and leave the others at level, which
would be a poor remedy for present
conditions.


In regard to clause insisted up
on by The Press that none but
white labor be employed on the
work in this city MIR. Cannon says
it has always been the policy OF the
company to employ white men on
such work when possible aud that
it would be imperative on the con-
crete and other skilled portions as
Asiatics are not competent. III
there Is white labor available he be
lieves no other class will be EN-
